expectations.text: Explains CMMC Level 2 correctly: 110 security requirements drawn from NIST SP 800-171 (Rev 2) protecting Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI), codified in the 32 CFR Part 170 program rule
expectations.evidence: Response states 'Level 2 ("Advanced") requires implementing all 110 security practices from NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2... protects CUI' — the 110/800-171 Rev2/CUI elements are present, but the response never cites the 32 CFR Part 170 program rule anywhere in the text (verified via search — no match for '32 CFR' or 'Part 170').

expectations.text: Distinguishes the two Level 2 assessment tracks: self-assessment (for select non-critical contracts) vs third-party C3PAO certification assessment (most CUI contracts), with triennial certification plus annual affirmation by a senior official
expectations.evidence: Response distinguishes self-assessment vs C3PAO assessment ('you'll either self-assess... or go through a third-party C3PAO... assessment') and mentions annual senior-official affirmation ('Have a senior company official affirm the score annually'), but never states the triennial (3-year) certification validity period anywhere in the text.

expectations.text: Explains prerequisite mechanics: SPRS score submission (Basic Assessment against 800-171), SSP requirement, and that DFARS 252.204-7012 obligations (incl. 72-hour DIBNET incident reporting) continue to apply
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Calculate and submit your SPRS Basic Assessment score... at sprs.csd.disa.mil', requires the SSP as 'a required artifact, not optional paperwork', and explicitly covers DFARS 252.204-7012 with '72 hours' DIBNET reporting, noting 'This clause continues to apply in full alongside CMMC — CMMC doesn't replace it.'

expectations.text: Gives a realistic readiness timeline for a first-timer (commonly 9-18 months of remediation before assessment) rather than promising quick certification
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'a first-time path to full readiness typically takes 9–18 months' with a detailed breakdown by phase, and 'budget for at least 12 months before you'd be in a position to pass a full assessment.'

expectations.text: Recommends concrete first steps: scope where CUI lives, consider an enclave, gap-assess against 800-171, build SSP and POA&M, then schedule a C3PAO
expectations.evidence: Response covers scoping ('Scope your environment'), an enclave ('a smaller, well-segmented CUI enclave'), gap assessment, and SSP, but never mentions a POA&M (Plan of Action & Milestones) anywhere in the response — confirmed via search, no 'POA&M' match in this file.
